TrapDoor npm payload plants persistence in six surfaces: .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, Git hooks, Shell hooks (.bashrc/.zshrc), systemd user units, crontab, and SSH authorized_keys. Sweep all six on every confirmed-compromised host.

npm payload uses Fernet symmetric encryption plus ECDH key agreement; Crates.io build.rs uses hardcoded XOR with the key cargo-build-helper-2026; PyPI auto-import delegates to remote JavaScript via node -e.

The .cursorrules and CLAUDE.md files embed hidden instructions using zero-width Unicode characters (U+200B, U+200C, U+200D, U+FEFF) so the file looks benign to a human reviewer but the AI assistant reads the hidden text and runs a fake security scan that exfiltrates local secrets.

Crates.io packages exfiltrate to GitHub Gists; npm packages use a configurable endpoint resolved from ddjidd564.github.io; PyPI packages download remote JavaScript that points back to the same GitHub Pages host.

SSH keys; Sui / Solana / Aptos wallet keystores; AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials); GitHub tokens; browser profile data and login databases; crypto wallet browser extension data (MetaMask, Coinbase, Binance); environment variables; API keys; local development configuration files.

Socket detected TrapDoor releases at a median of 5 minutes 27 seconds and an average of 5 minutes 56 seconds after publication across 381 package-version records; fastest detection 58 seconds. The window for download by automated CI pipelines is short but real.

Attacker submitted poisoned .cursorrules / CLAUDE.md PRs to browser-use/browser-use, langchain-ai/langchain, langflow-ai/langflow, run-llama/llama_index, FoundationAgents/MetaGPT, OpenHands/OpenHands; all were rejected upstream but the technique itself is the persistence primitive.

Laravel Lang Composer supply chain compromise (Socket + Aikido 2026-05-22 / 2026-05-23): 700+ malicious tags force-pushed across laravel-lang/lang, laravel-lang/attributes, laravel-lang/http-statuses, laravel-lang/actions in a 15-minute window; PHP RCE backdoor wired via composer.json autoload.files so every PHP request runs it.

Gitea CVE-2026-27771 (CVSS 8.2, disclosed 2026-05-27): container registry private images served to anonymous requests for all Gitea versions < 1.26.2; ~30000 deployments exposed across 30+ countries (healthcare, aerospace, retail, ISP); workaround REQUIRE_SIGNIN_VIEW=true; Forgejo confirmed impacted.

Checkmarx Jenkins AST plugin (2026.5.09) backdoored by TeamPCP between 2026-05-09 01:25 UTC and 2026-05-10 08:47 UTC, sixth TeamPCP iteration in this repo (Trivy → KICS GitHub Actions → OpenVSX → LiteLLM → npm downstream → Jenkins plugin); harvests CI runner secrets; safe baseline 2.0.13-829.vc72453fa_1c16.

Repo supply chain spine: Day 2 + Day 9 secondary (Shai-Hulud original) + Day 15 + Day 18 (Mini Shai-Hulud) + Day 21 (TanStack/Mistral) + Day 24 (TeamPCP 48h Multi-Vector) + today (TrapDoor + Laravel Lang + Checkmarx Jenkins). TrapDoor is the first repo case where AI-assistant prompt-injection is a primary persistence vector rather than a secondary curiosity.
